name: Reactive Dependency Tracer
short_description: Extracts the graph of reactive data-flow dependencies invisible at compile time: SwiftUI @Published/@EnvironmentObject/@StateObject observation links, Combine operator pipelines, and Jetpack Compose snapshot-based state reads. Produces a directed :ReactiveEdge graph from state-producers to state-consumers so palace agents can answer 'what re-renders when X changes?'
tech_stack: swift-syntax (SwiftSyntax SPM library) — AST traversal to locate @Published, @StateObject, @EnvironmentObject, @ObservationIgnored, .sink/.receive/.assign Combine operators, and @Observable (Swift 5.9 Observation macro)
tree-sitter (tree-sitter-swift grammar) — fallback parser; handles incremental reparsing after edits
Kotlin compiler plugin (kotlinx.ast or detekt AST) — traversal of collectAsState(), collectLatest(), StateFlow.value reads, MutableStateFlow emissions, remember{}/derivedStateOf{} blocks
Compose Stability Analyzer (skydoves/compose-stability-analyzer) — Compose compiler reports stability classification
SourceKit-LSP (indexstore-db) — type-resolves @Published property owner type, resolves ObservableObject conformances across module boundaries
semgrep (Swift experimental + Kotlin beta) — lightweight heuristic pass for custom ObservableObject subclass detection
entity_model: Node types: :ReactiveSource (name, kind=[Published|StateFlow|MutableStateFlow|ObservableObject|SharedFlow|State|DerivedState], owner_symbol, module, path, framework=[SwiftUI|Combine|Compose|Custom]). :ReactiveConsumer (name, kind=[View|ViewModel|Presenter|Composable|Subscriber], symbol, module, path). :ReactivePipeline (id, operators=[map|filter|flatMap|debounce|...], source_symbol, sink_symbol). :StateKey (name, type_fqn, scope). Edge types: :OBSERVES (ReactiveConsumer→ReactiveSource, properties=[String], is_conditional=bool). :FEEDS_PIPELINE. :PIPELINE_DELIVERS_TO. :ENVIRONMENT_INJECTS. Faceted axes: structural=reactive-flow, semantic-kind=[publisher|subscriber|pipeline|environment-slot], domain-concept=[state-management|data-binding], capability=[Observes|Emits|Transforms].

prior_art.name: swiftui-atom-properties (ra1028)
prior_art.notes: graphDescription() DOT output of atomic state dependency graph at runtime. MIT. v1.3.3 (2024-09).

prior_art.name: Compose Stability Analyzer (skydoves)
prior_art.notes: Real-time IntelliJ plugin showing Compose composable stability. Apache-2.0. Active 2025.

prior_art.name: XCGrapher (maxchuquimia)
prior_art.notes: Xcode framework-level dependency graph generator. MIT, last commit 2024.

prior_art.name: RepoGraph (arXiv 2410.14684)
prior_art.notes: Repository-level code graphs; cross-file call edges.

prior_art.name: semgrep Swift experimental support
prior_art.notes: Swift support remains experimental as of 2025. Cross-module type resolution limited.

consumer_query_patterns.label: All views that re-render when a given StateFlow changes
consumer_query_patterns.cypher: MATCH (src:ReactiveSource {name: $stateflow_name, module: $module})<-[:OBSERVES|FEEDS_PIPELINE*1..3]-(consumer:ReactiveConsumer) RETURN consumer.symbol, consumer.kind, consumer.path ORDER BY consumer.module

consumer_query_patterns.label: Full Combine pipeline from source to terminal sink
consumer_query_patterns.cypher: MATCH path=(src:ReactiveSource)-[:FEEDS_PIPELINE]->(pipe:ReactivePipeline)-[:PIPELINE_DELIVERS_TO]->(sink:ReactiveConsumer) WHERE src.name = $publisher_name RETURN [n IN nodes(path) | n.name] AS pipeline_chain, pipe.operators

consumer_query_patterns.label: All @EnvironmentObject injection sites for a given type
consumer_query_patterns.cypher: MATCH (src:ReactiveSource {kind: 'ObservableObject'})-[:ENVIRONMENT_INJECTS]->(consumer:ReactiveConsumer) WHERE src.owner_symbol CONTAINS $type_name RETURN consumer.symbol, consumer.path ORDER BY consumer.module

consumer_query_patterns.label: Modules with the highest reactive fan-out (state explosion risk)
consumer_query_patterns.cypher: MATCH (src:ReactiveSource)<-[:OBSERVES]-(c:ReactiveConsumer) WITH src.module AS mod, count(DISTINCT c) AS observer_count ORDER BY observer_count DESC LIMIT 10 RETURN mod, observer_count

graphiti_episode_type: EntityNode for :ReactiveSource and :ReactiveConsumer. EpisodicNode for :ReactivePipeline (volatile — operator chain changes frequently). Edges :OBSERVES carry valid_from/valid_until. :ENVIRONMENT_INJECTS edges permanent while injection site exists.
ingest_perf_est: [uncertain] Full ingest on 20-Kit repo: estimated 8-20 minutes. Swift-syntax AST traversal ~2-5 min. Kotlin compiler plugin Gradle path ~5-15 min. Incremental: ~30-90 seconds per changed file.
failure_modes: False negatives for @Observable macro (Swift 5.9+) — macro expansion post-parse; swift-syntax sees pre-expansion source
Indirect Combine pipelines stored in AnyCancellable bags across modules — sink visible but chain source may be in another module
Compose derivedStateOf{} blocks reading multiple upstream states create implicit multi-fan-in edges
SwiftUI @Binding creates implicit two-way reactive coupling not captured by publisher-centric analysis
Generated reactive code (macros, code-gen) visible only as generated file
Swift 6 actor isolation on @Published properties requires actor-aware traversal
Kotlin compiler plugin path requires Gradle daemon warm-up (4-8 GB heap for large builds)
open_questions: How to handle @Observable (Swift 5.9 Observation framework) vs legacy @Published/@ObservableObject? Macro expands to invisible _$observationRegistrar calls.
Compose snapshot tracking is runtime mechanism (SlotTable). Should Item 3 integrate with Compose Stability Analyzer compiler reports?
Separation of concerns with Item 14 (Lifecycle & State): both model @StateObject/@EnvironmentObject. Shared :ReactiveSource node type needed?
Incremental trigger granularity: file-level for Kotlin acceptable or needs Gradle build pass?
Should cross-module @EnvironmentObject injection be modelled as graph edge given runtime-only propagation?
adversarial_resistance: low — macro-expanded reactive patterns hide actual state wiring from source-level AST. Confidence should be reduced to 0.5 for macro-heavy modules.
